text: If I keep going, my English will grow.
If I had more time, I would learn the piano.
Imagine
Suppose
Wish
Would rather
Hesitate
Opportunity
Regret
Pretend
If I won the lottery, I would build a school.
If I were younger, I would study a new career.
If I had the chance, I would not hesitate.
Sandra, suppose you had a whole free year. What would you do?
If I had a free year, I would travel to Japan. I would not hesitate for a second.
Nice. And if you were younger, would you choose a different career?
Honestly, no. I have no regrets. But I would rather learn something new every year than stop.
And if someone offered you the chance to live abroad, would you take it?
If I had that opportunity, I would take it, even if it felt a little scary. I love to imagine new things.
If I had no limits, I would
Suppose I were free for a year, I would
If I had the opportunity, I would rather
If I had more time, I would travel.
If I were younger, I would study something new.
I would rather dream big than sit still.
If I had the opportunity, I would not hesitate.
I have no regrets, but I love to imagine new things.

Let me tell you about a little daydream of mine. If I had more free time, I would learn to play the piano. I have always wanted to, but life is busy. If I were a bit braver, I would take a long trip around South America, just me and a backpack. And if I won the lottery tomorrow, I would not stop working, but I would build a small school for languages in my hometown. Of course, these are only imaginary situations. I do not really have the time or the money right now. But I think it is healthy to imagine. If you never picture a different life, you never change anything. So I like to suppose, to wonder, to ask myself: what would I do if things were different? Sometimes the daydream slowly becomes a real plan.

I want to share what I would do in a few imaginary situations. If I had a free year, I would travel to Japan to see where part of my family comes from. I would not hesitate; I would book the trip right away. If I were younger, maybe I would study a completely new career, but honestly, I have no regrets about my path. And if someone offered me the chance to live abroad for a while, I would take the opportunity, even if it felt a little scary. I am 51, and I still love to imagine new things. I would rather dream big and try than sit still and wonder. If I did not keep dreaming, learning English would feel pointless. But every new word opens a door, so I keep going.

First, imagine a situation that is not real right now, like winning the lottery. Then, start your sentence with if and a past verb, to show it is imaginary. Next, say what you would do, using would plus a verb. After that, add a short reason, so your idea is clear. Finally, you can switch the order and put the would part first, if it sounds more natural to you.
Imagine
Suppose
Wish
Would rather
Hesitate
Opportunity
Regret
Pretend
If I had more time, I would travel.
If I won the lottery, I would build a school.
If I were younger, I would study art.
Suppose you won a prize, what would you do?
If I had the chance, I would not hesitate.
I would rather travel than stay home.
